back_translation: The Lord called Moses, and spoke to him from the tent of meeting, saying,

Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When any one among you brings an offering to present to the Lord, you shall present your offering from livestock, namely from the herd of cattle, or from the flock of small animals.

If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd of cattle, he must present a male without blemish; he shall bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, to be pleasing before the face of the Lord.
He shall lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering, and that offering shall be pleasing for him, to make atonement for him.

And he shall kill the son of the herd before the face of the Lord, then the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall present the blood and sprinkle the blood on all sides of the altar at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
And he shall skin the burnt offering and cut it into pieces.
And the sons of Aaron the priest shall place fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire.
And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall arrange the pieces of meat, both the head and the fat, on the wood that is on the fire on the altar.
And he shall wash the entrails and legs with water, and the priest shall burn all of it on the altar as a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
If his offering is a burnt offering from the flock of small animals, namely from sheep or from goats, he must present a male without blemish.
And he shall kill it on the north side of the altar, before the face of the Lord, and the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall sprinkle its blood on all sides of the altar.
And he shall cut it into pieces, together with its head and its fat, and the priest shall arrange these on the wood that is on the fire on the altar.
And he shall wash the entrails and legs with water, and the priest shall present all of it and burn it on the altar, as a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
And if his offering to the Lord is a burnt offering from birds, he shall present his offering from turtledoves or from sons of the pigeon.
And the priest shall bring it to the altar, twist its neck, and burn it to smoke on the altar, and its blood shall be drained at the side of the altar.
And he shall remove its crop together with its feathers, and throw it beside the altar on the east side, at the place designated for ashes.

He shall tear the bird at its wings, but shall not separate it apart, and the priest shall burn it to smoke on the altar, on the wood that is on the fire; it is a burnt offering, an offering by fire, a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
